texto: When tragedy comes like this, at first it is complete. You do not need to think it over, or decide what it means. For it is far ahead of you, and the very act of acknowledging it means letting it go. But then it comes round again - and it goes through you and is worse than before.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Benjamin Lytal: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “When tragedy comes like this, at first it is complete. You do not need to think it over, or decid…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death, grief-and-loss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e grief-and-loss.
